justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Optimistic locking used with updateSeatStatus called with expectedCurrentStatus='available' (CAS pattern). (2) False return from updateSeatStatus correctly throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. (3) AC3 tested with Promise.allSettled simulating concurrent calls where one gets true and the other gets false. (4) AC4 tested with findSeat returning null -> SEAT_NOT_FOUND. (5) Confidence section acknowledges AC3 guarantee depends on DB implementation being truly atomic, which is the concurrency testing limitation. (6) All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage.

observations.text: Correct CAS/optimistic-locking pattern with expectedCurrentStatus='available' — no naive check-then-act
AC3 tested with Promise.allSettled proving exactly 1 fulfilled + 1 rejected, simulating the race via mock returning true then false
Low confidence area correctly notes real-world AC3 depends on DB atomicity — acknowledges unit tests alone cannot fully verify concurrency
Fast-path rejection in AC2 avoids unnecessary write attempts when seat is already reserved at read time
10 tests across all 4 ACs with thorough coverage including error shape verification
